metadata.title: WHO-Specific Regulatory Requirements Diverging from ICH
sources: WHO TRS 1010 Annex 10 - Stability Testing of APIs and FPPs (2018)
WHO TRS 986 Annex 2 - GMP for Pharmaceutical Products (2014)

climatic_zones.description: WHO defines climatic zones for stability testing that go beyond ICH Q1A(R2) which only covers Zones I and II. ICH Q1F was withdrawn, leaving Zones III/IV to WHO and regional authorities.

ich_q1f_withdrawal.background: ICH Q1F originally defined 30C/65% RH for Climatic Zones III/IV. Some Zone IV countries wanted stricter conditions (30C/75% RH). Due to this divergence, ICH withdrew Q1F and delegated Zone III/IV conditions to WHO and regional authorities.
ich_q1f_withdrawal.consequence: WHO now sets the definitive global stability conditions for hot/humid climates not covered by ICH

zone_III.countries_example: Iraq, Sudan, parts of Middle East

zone_IVa.countries_example: Iran, Egypt, parts of Southeast Asia

zone_IVb.countries_example: Brazil, Ghana, Indonesia, Philippines, Tanzania, Nigeria

long_term.minimum_duration: 12 months or 6 months as described in section 2.1.7
long_term.note: Condition selected based on climatic zone where API is intended to be stored

intermediate.note: If 30C/65% RH or 30C/75% RH is the long-term condition, there is no intermediate condition

api_freezer.note: No accelerated condition defined; single batch at elevated temperature may be used

long_term.minimum_duration_new_api: 12 months or as described in section 2.2.7

fpp_semi_permeable_containers.description: Aqueous products in semi-permeable containers require low-RH testing to evaluate water loss
fpp_semi_permeable_containers.water_loss_threshold: 5% loss in water from initial value is significant change
fpp_semi_permeable_containers.who_unique: This low-RH testing regime for semi-permeable containers is a WHO-specific elaboration not found in ICH Q1A

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1F (now withdrawn) defined 30C/65% RH for all Zone III/IV
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO defines Zone IVb at 30C/75% RH, a more stringent humidity requirement
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Products for Zone IVb markets must test at higher humidity; data from Zone II studies may be insufficient

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1A always requires intermediate testing consideration
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: When long-term condition is 30C/65% or 30C/75%, no intermediate condition exists
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Fewer study arms required for Zone IVa/IVb products

who_vs_ich_divergences.area: Batch Requirements for Generics (FPPs with existing APIs)
who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1A requires 3 batches for all initial submissions
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO allows 2 batches for uncomplicated FPPs with existing APIs (at least pilot scale); 3rd batch may be smaller
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Reduced batch requirement for generic manufacturers targeting WHO prequalification

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1A provides limited guidance on semi-permeable containers
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO provides detailed low-RH testing protocol with water loss rate ratio calculations and 5% threshold
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: WHO-specific methodology for aqueous products in HDPE, PET containers

who_vs_ich_divergences.area: Minimum Data at Submission for Existing APIs
who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH requires minimum 12 months long-term data
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO allows 6 months minimum for FPPs containing existing APIs

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1A defines significant change generally
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO explicitly defines: 5% change in API content by assay, any degradation product exceeding criterion, failure of appearance/functionality tests, pH criterion failure, dissolution failure for 12 dosage units
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: More prescriptive criteria for triggering intermediate testing

who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO mandates at least 1 production batch/year added to monitoring, tested every 6 months year 1 then annually; OOS results must be reported to finished product manufacturer and competent authorities
WHO prohibits terms like 'ambient conditions' or 'room temperature'; requires specific storage statements from defined list in Appendix 2
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Stricter labelling requirements for WHO-prequalified products

who_vs_ich_divergences.area: Zone II Data for Zone IV Distribution
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: Appendix 3 provides specific interpretation for products approved in Zone II but distributed in Zone IV, including additional testing requirements
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Products approved in temperate climates need additional stability justification for tropical markets

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH Q1A provides general stress testing guidance
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO specifies 10C increments (50C, 60C), 75% RH or greater for humidity stress, and mandates photolysis across pH range for solutions/suspensions

who_vs_ich_divergences.area: Retest Period vs Shelf Life Distinction
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO explicitly states APIs get 'retest period' (can be retested and reused if compliant) while FPPs get 'shelf life' (absolute expiry); APIs can be retested multiple times
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Clearer operational distinction for API manufacturers

who_vs_ich_divergences.ich_position: ICH requires commitment if data does not cover proposed shelf life
who_vs_ich_divergences.who_position: WHO provides tiered commitment requirements: 3 production batches = no commitment; <3 batches = continue + add to total of 3; no production batch data = place first 3 production batches on stability
who_vs_ich_divergences.impact: Explicit commitment tiers for WHO prequalification

who_gmp_unique_requirements.description: WHO GMP (TRS 986 Annex 2) supplements ICH Q7 with requirements specific to resource-limited settings and WHO prequalification

key_differences.who_detail: Japan and EU historically accepted doses at 100x clinical dose (mg/kg), while US traditionally accepted only MTD-based dose selection. ICH S1C harmonized around MTD with AUC ratio alternatives.
key_differences.japan_specific: Japan defined high dose as causing no more than 10% body weight suppression vs control, no poisoning deaths, and no serious toxicity findings
key_differences.eu_specific: EU CPMP defined high dose as producing mild toxic effects (e.g., 10% body weight suppression or mild organ toxicity)
key_differences.harmonized: Minimum AUC ratio of 25x proposed as pharmacokinetic metric

key_differences.who_detail: Not WHO-specific but PMDA requires 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' status under PAL Article 13-3 for any foreign manufacturer exporting drugs to Japan. 5-year renewal cycle.
key_differences.impact: Additional site registration burden beyond FDA establishment registration or EU MIA

who_prequalification_context.description: WHO stability guidelines are primarily designed for the WHO Prequalification Programme for multisource/generic products
applicable_to: Generic pharmaceutical products seeking WHO prequalification
APIs supplied to WHO-prequalified FPP manufacturers
Products distributed through UN agencies (UNICEF, UNFPA, Global Fund)
National regulatory authorities using WHO guidelines as reference
who_prequalification_context.relationship_to_ich: WHO guidelines cross-reference ICH Q1A(R2), Q1B, Q1C, Q1D, Q1E, Q5C but add requirements for climatic zones III/IV and resource-limited settings
